danger_signals.visual_form: Sharp spike obstacles embedded in terrain and ceilings
danger_signals.meaning: Instant damage hazard; contact causes life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Extended underwater passages (e.g., Hydrocity zone); timer pressure
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threat; player must surface or exit within time limit or lose life

danger_signals.visual_form: Off-screen void at level boundaries and drops
danger_signals.meaning: Fatal fall; triggers immediate life loss and checkpoint respawn

danger_signals.visual_form: Mechanical enemy sprites patrolling zones; variety of designs per zone theme
danger_signals.meaning: Encounter hazard; collision without rings causes damage; can be defeated by jump or spin dash

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, heavily armored Robotnik-piloted machines at end of acts; distinct silhouettes
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat; requires pattern recognition and multi-hit defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Rings scatter visually from player on hit; visual count decreases
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability escalation; loss of all rings = next hit causes life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen countdown timer; accelerates urgency in final minute
danger_signals.meaning: Pressure mechanic; exceeding limit results in life loss and checkpoint reset

safety_signals.visual_form: Scattered, shimmering ring sprites throughout level geometry
safety_signals.meaning: Health system; collecting grants damage absorption (can take one hit without life loss)

safety_signals.visual_form: Post-like markers passed during level progression; visual activation on contact
safety_signals.meaning: Save point; defines respawn location on life loss; reaching new level auto-saves to slot

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing TV monitor sprites with distinct pictograms (shield, invincibility, rings, extra life)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary buffs; grants temporary invincibility, elemental shields, ring collection, or extra life

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual aura around character (fire shield, water shield, ring-attract shield)
safety_signals.meaning: Damage mitigation; allows underwater breathing, fire resistance, or automatic ring magnetism

safety_signals.visual_form: Curved track structures in level design; allows high-speed flow
safety_signals.meaning: Momentum mechanics; provide pathways and speed bonus opportunities

safety_signals.visual_form: Giant rings in secret passages; gumball machine corridor visualization
safety_signals.meaning: Reward engagement; accessing 50+ rings at checkpoint warps to power-up collection stage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Robotnik's Death Egg space station; his large mecha robots as visual proxy
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Badniks throughout zones; zone-specific robot designs (e.g., aquatic badniks in Hydrocity); boss mechas piloted by Robotnik with distinctive silhouettes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Industrial, mechanical architecture; metallic hazards and trap placement; boss chambers styled as Robotnik facilities

faction_indicators.color_identity: Sonic: blue with red shoes; Tails: orange/yellow with white
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character sprite identity; Chaos Emeralds as collectible objective
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Natural, organic level themes (Angel Island, water zones); treasure/ring placement suggesting freedom and exploration

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red knuckles and chest stripe; brown/tan body
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Island guardian status; lays traps for Sonic and Tails on Angel Island
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Knuckles as independent obstacle and trap-setter; visually distinct from badniks
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Angel Island locale; booby traps and environmental hazards aligned with Knuckles' intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Organic terrain with natural elements; tropical and earthen color palette; clear sky and natural lighting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Badniks patrol predictably; Knuckles actively guards; level progression flows naturally through zone themes

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Death Egg crash-landed on Angel Island
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Industrial metallic structures appear in sky and terrain; mechanical elements intrude into natural zones; color shift toward grays and silvers in later acts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased robot presence; Knuckles becomes obstacle to Sonic's progress; bosses escalate in sophistication

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprite shows no ring aura; HUD displays zero ring count
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Next collision with any hazard triggers immediate life loss; increased defensive play required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Golden coloration; character sprite transformation; potential invincibility visual effect
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unlocks post-credits scene; indicates 100% completion achievement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cyan/blue tint overlay; bubbles and water particle effects; character movement slowed visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Drowning timer activates; Tails can swim freely; Sonic requires shield to breathe; physics altered for buoyancy

environmental_storytelling.example: Angel Island (Act 1-2): Tropical, organic; Hydrocity Zone: Flooded underwater passages; industrial zones reflect Robotnik facility takeover
Robot enemies styled to match zone environment (aquatic badniks in water zones, mechanical sentries in industrial areas); communicates Robotnik's adaptation to terrain
Early zones show natural structures; later zones increasingly dominated by Robotnik's metallic machinery, visually narrating his invasion of Angel Island
Rings cluster along risky paths and dangerous jumps, creating visual incentive/reward for skilled play and risk-taking
Each boss arena reflects the zone's theme while showing Robotnik's technological superiority; final boss arena reflects Death Egg's scale
Passing checkpoints provides visual confirmation of progress and safety anchor; communicates level structure through placement

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Blue blur effect; rapid sprite animation; visual momentum carry through loops and curves
Auditory chime; visual sparkle effect on character; ring count HUD increments
Knockback animation; character sprite flash/flicker; rings scatter outward in burst pattern
Tail-spinning animation; character rises above Sonic; can traverse gaps independently
Different movement animation; can navigate water without timer pressure unlike Sonic
Predictable left-right or circular movement patterns; telegraphed collision zones
Explosion effect; sprite dissolution; point value display; auditory confirmation
Stationary or patrol behavior in specific zones; active trap-setting; pursues player when provoked
Multi-phase attack patterns; visual damage states (armor reduction, coloration change); telegraphed attack tells (pause before projectile, wind-up animations)
Screen flash; item sprite emerges; auditory fanfare; temporary visual effect on player (e.g., shield glow, invincibility stars)

notes: Sonic 3 communicates world state primarily through visual theming and environmental design rather than explicit UI. Danger is signaled through familiar platformer conventions (spikes, water, pits) and robot enemy presence. Safety is established through ring collection, checkpoint placement, and power-up accessibility. The game's core storytelling—Robotnik's invasion of Angel Island via the Death Egg—is shown through gradual visual shift from organic natural terrain to industrial mechanical structures. Character abilities (Sonic's speed, Tails' flight/swimming) are demonstrated through gameplay rather than narrative exposition. The separation of Sonic 3 and Sonic & Knuckles for cartridge economics means narrative and world-state progression is incomplete in standalone Sonic 3; the combined 'Sonic 3 & Knuckles' represents the intended full experience.
